us-bibliographic-data-grant.invention-title: Wireless charging apparatus with adaptive power control

abstract.p: A wireless charging apparatus comprises a transmitter coil configured to generate an alternating magnetic field and a power control circuit adapted to dynamically adjust transmitted power based on receiver feedback. The apparatus enables efficient inductive energy transfer without physical contact.

claim.claim-text: 1. A wireless charging apparatus comprising: a transmitter coil configured to generate an alternating magnetic field at a resonant frequency; and a power control circuit operably coupled to the transmitter coil, the power control circuit adapted to dynamically adjust a transmitted power level based on feedback received from a receiver device.
2. The wireless charging apparatus of claim 1, wherein the resonant frequency is between 100 kHz and 200 kHz.
3. The wireless charging apparatus of claim 1, wherein the power control circuit comprises a microcontroller programmed to execute a power optimization algorithm.

us-bibliographic-data-grant.invention-title: Receiver coil alignment system for wireless power transfer

abstract.p: A receiver coil alignment system detects misalignment between transmitter and receiver coils in a wireless power transfer arrangement. An alignment correction mechanism repositions the receiver coil to maximize power transfer efficiency. The system includes magnetic field sensors and a feedback loop for real-time adjustment.

claim.claim-text: 1. A receiver coil alignment system for wireless power transfer, the system comprising: a receiver coil configured to receive power via inductive coupling; a plurality of magnetic field sensors disposed around a perimeter of the receiver coil; and an alignment controller coupled to the sensors and configured to detect coil misalignment and actuate a positioning mechanism to correct the misalignment.
2. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of magnetic field sensors comprises at least four Hall-effect sensors arranged in a quadrilateral pattern.
